title: Chromolaena ulei
taxonomy: Chromolaena ulei is classified within the family Asteraceae. This classification places it within the larger group of daisy and sunflower plants. intro: Chromolaena ulei, also known as Brazilian mullein, is a plant species in the sunflower family native to South America, particularly in Brazil and Peru. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, making it beneficial in treating various health conditions such as pain, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Chromolaena ulei include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
